item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: This image is property of Tim Walker, whose website strictly disallows commercial usage of their images. As such, the image is not compliant with CC 3.0 and has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Realistic interview along with a good anomaly to bring it all together.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That 11% who don't have depression afterwards were already hyper Calvinists, who basically believe this already :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That interview log shattered my heart into a zillion or so pieces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really can't see why kid was designated D-class, not SCP-1432-1 (or SCP-1432-whatever).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The part that was most terrifying for me was the possibility that he was right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason, this reminds me of The Ring. Besides that, I think the "look at something and start to believe you are some other object" idea is overdone. For that, -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also liked it until the interview. It really didn't add any extra creep or horror or 'sucker punch' feeling that the note about initial recovery being from the home of a 12 year old who had killed his parents didn't. It felt a little mawkish in places, too, and I don't think the Foundation would see the need to include a note on the researcher requesting an amnestic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I had seen this sooner, I really liked it until I hit the interview which just did not come across as believable to me at all, not from a twelve year old. No vote from me, but I love the picture and DVD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know if this was intended as meta, but I like it because I read it that way.

The video made little Billy think that he and everyone he knows are artificial constructs with artificial personalities being made to jump through hoops for the amusement of some unknown controller. Thing is, they are. Danielsm created them all from nothing, and they exist solely for our entertainment.

The dialog in the interview seems a bit too mature and sophisticated for a 12 year old, but not so much that it spoiled my enjoyment. +1

Afterthought on containment: Why are we keeping every copy we find? For that matter, why keep any physical copies at all? The bit about monitoring the internet would imply that the physical media is utterly irrelevant, why not save space/money and make security easier by keeping a digital copy on a secured and backed-up server?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I edited it to address your middle point. As of now, the Foundation is officially unclear on whether the compulsion to kill is another effect of the DVD or a result of having your worldview radically altered.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah i had to chuckle at that point , because someone had a storie where c is an bullet to the head and the doctor would in that case be a bit … well

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, okay. Thanks for the correction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the meat of the article, but I do have a few problems with the stuff around it. The opening sentence of the article refers to "the containment chamber" without mentioning a containment chamber before, and the specifications of this containment chamber are never revealed. I also don't like the interview, it's meant to be with a 12 year old child, but it just comes across as being with a generic delusional adult. The idea of the interview isn't necessarily bad, but it feels poorly written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: *puts on Mod Pants* Dmatix is mostly correct. The official policy is that you can use any letter you want to mean whatever strength of amnestic you want, because There Is No Canon. A lot of people use A as the strongest, and that's the usage I see most, although there are some people who use it the other way around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of all, congrats on your first article. =)

As for my opinion on the piece, the tone's great, and I like the new ending of the vid, but there's a few things that feel a little off:

  • People exposed to it are reclassified as D-class instead of as SCP-1432-1, as victims of most other permanently-people-altering SCPs tend to be
  • It could stand to be made more explicit that the urge to take people apart as proof is part of the video's effects, rather than logically following from this "revelation". *is a cheerful existential nihilist, for the record*
  • Researcher asking for an amnestic at the end

That said, I like it more than I dislike it, so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While there is no actual canon for that, you have the general view of things backwards: Class A are the strongest kind, C are the weakest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 overall. Solid first SCP.

One quibble:

Class C amnesiacs are a full wipe. I think class A would be a lot more appropriate.

For the record (from what I've come to understand, might be wrong):
Class A - short term memory wipe
Class B - Long term memory wipe
Class C - Full sweep

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think the interview actually adds anything to the article. Besides the interview it's very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a few iffy tone bits (such as "non-psychopathic subjects") but I like this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I initially hoped that this was causing a minor reality-bending effect so that the dismembered people actually were artificial, "proving" the affected person's point. This is well done, so upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's what they WANT you to think!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …aren't all D-Class differing levels of violent and insane? I mean, we get them from death row, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, it makes sense now. I shall upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Opposite of Skara Brae, I think the effect is great, but the item is kinda eh. The story you weave via the effects and the interview I felt were compelling, but that this is caused by something so obviously related feels cheap. Granted, without some sort of anomalous object to cause it, you'd pretty much just have a creepypasta, not that there's anything wrong with that.

No vote now, but I may change that later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great tone and organization for a first article, but I'm downvoting on the concept— specifically the effect. Not every SCP needs to be creepy, but when creepy is the intent I expect actual creepiness.

The image and the lead-in story were both decent (even if creepy dolls and DVDs what make you go nuts have both been rather done to death), but the "we're all dolls" effect is just too cliche and boring. I also thought the progression into insanity was a bit nonsensical— maybe it was just the way it was described. Why do people care if they're dolls if everyone is a doll? Matrix? What internal transmogrification is going on, exactly?

I dunno. Aside from the cliches, I know determinists and Calvinists who walk around feeling like this all the time, so the whole compulsive murder thing falls flat, for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My meaning was that all people who weren't psychopaths would experience emotional distress when murdering people at the SCP's compulsion. Presumably, since psychopaths wouldn't care about murdering people prior to exposure to the SCP, they wouldn't care about doing so after exposure either. I can see where its ambiguous though, I'll try to rephrase.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was just flipping through the SCP-fuel pages and it creeped me out more than a lot of the others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea, but that is not what psychopathy is. Look it up on Wikipedia or something. No vote until that is fixed.

item.title: Re: New video ending
item.content:encoded: I like it. I didn't mind the previous version, but the new one fits the effect much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is overly good. I'm still wondering where the hell you would find a picture like that.

item.title: Re: yeesh
item.content:encoded: Thank you! ;)

item.title: New video ending
item.content:encoded: Ok changed the ending of the video to something I thought was more creepy. Let me know if you think it's good or not, or if you have any alternate endings in mind. For comparison's sake, the previous ending was that the doll corners the girl and breaks her neck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some others mentioned the video ending being kind of lame as well…I'll try to think up something better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Fixed that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are some problems, but I like it enough to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, having JUST read Kurt Vonnegut's Breakfast of Champions (for those not in the know-there's the idea in the book that only one person on earth really has free will, and everyone else is a robot designed to get a reaction from him), this kinda creeps me out. Unfortunately, it's not great, but I like it. just a little more believability in the containment procedures, a different end to the video (word-for-word though; "okay, big doll chasing a woman…creepy. breaks neck 173?"), and this could be awesome. I would like to have this actually be memetic (it's a favorite idea of mine), but yeah, it's just mind-affecting, and probably good to keep it like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Police station communications are to be monitored for murders involving an unusual DVD.

Is this something that would normally be reported or even noted?

I would imagine that some of the murderers would mention the DVD during interrogation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting start, but major issue: As described, this is not a memetic agent. Mind-affecting? Yes. Cognitohazard? Yes. Memetic? It doesn't spread person-to-person, nor are the affected individuals capable of reproducing the infecting agent. As such, it doesn't qualify.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good first skip, but there are a few issues. How would the police know about the DVD? Why would the Foundation use insane and violent subjects as D-Class? They would probably either kill them or imprison them. Tenative +1 for now.

item.title: yeesh
item.content:encoded: you really took the "think of what scares you most" idea in the rr seriously

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview dialogue is kinda off, but overall I liked it. Good job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the comments everyone! Have to go now, but will be back later to edit more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This isn't terrible, but there are some issues with this that keep me from upvoting.

Detailed Comments Episode IV
A New Hope

Police station communications are to be monitored for murders involving an unusual DVD.

Is this something that would normally be reported or even noted? I don't really see how the DVD would factor in to anything the police were doing.

any individual affected by SCP-1432 is to be reclassified as a D-class personnel and placed in solitary confinement

Being made a D-Class is something that isn't generally looked upon in a favorable light anymore, I feel like just saying they're to be isolated would be enough

SCP-##

Might want to fix that.

generic DVD’s

Generic seems like an odd word choice to me, but it isn't really incorrect

and breaks her neck

You had a really nice buildup, explaining the creepy size changing doll chasing the woman. It was so nice that I think "breaks her neck" is a really lame payoff. Something more anomalous would fit, I think. Maybe even something like "the video cuts to black for approximately 17 seconds, before returning to a *something creepy done to the woman*"

That's just my opinion though, others may feel differently.

This effect has proven immune to all amnesiacs currently in the Foundation’s possession

Seems like weird phrasing to me, but is not technically wrong.

In addition, 73% of subjects will kill at least one other individual.

This is really off-putting to me. How is this measured? Does the Foundation just let affected subjects roam around for a bit and see if they murder anyone? I think it would be better to say that they will attempt violent action against another individual.

a police station in [DATA EXPUNGED], Ohio on 11/5/[DATA EXPUNGED]

You could probably just use plain blackboxing here.

Interviewer: Researcher [DATA EXPUNGED]

Also here.

The interview stuff

Pretty good, no glaring issues, except for;

Researcher [DATA EXPUNGED] subsequently requested and received a Class C Amnesiac.

This is just mostly grating against my own headcanon, but I don't think what happened in the interview was substantial or disturbing enough to shake a Foundation Researcher badly enough to request amnestics.

Overall I think you have potential, fix it up some and I'll likely upvote.

EDIT: Apparently I'm typing super slow today and others beat me to a few of my comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice first SCP. Have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually kinda like this. +1… Although, there are a few minor discrepancies with the format, and one thing that really stands out (to me) in the containment procedures.

Recovered instances of SCP-1432 are to be placed in a cabinet in the containment chamber

Just a cabinet? Not something like a lock box or something? Or maybe you meant a filing cabinet. I’d specify that. Also Researcher [DATA EXPUNGED] should be something like Researcher █████. Nice Job! :3

E: Oh. Wow. Four people before me said almost exactly what I just said while typing this. O_0

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, not very used to wiki stuff yet- I really do appreciate your help. Jaquerel just gave me advice on using the black box, I'll try that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this has some definite potential for creep, but the way it actually goes about the effect is kinda boring. If you could make the bit where they try to find the dolls and kill people more interesting, I'd upvote. Also, the interview didn't do anything for me, it has pretty bad dialog/expungement and doesn't really add to the article overall.

Also, here are the blackbox characters:████

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Good picture, good trigger, good effect, I like it, upvoted.

Changing [DATA EXPUNGED] to ███████ would improve readability. And maybe placing the whole interview into a blockquote too.

Most of the interview reads appropriately for a 12-year old, but some phrases strike me as a bit off: "I mean, it was obvious, how could I not have seen it sooner?", "all part of someone’s messed up game.", "They’re so fucking clever.".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On keyboards, hold down "ALT," type in "219," then let go of "ALT."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I fixed it for ya. You didn't space it out correctly or use the dialog boxes, as were given in the template in "How to Write an SCP."

As for the expungement, you seem to expunge weird things (which could be partially in part to my headcanon of ████=just that, [REDACTED]=kept out of that particular copy, [DATA EXPUNGED]=Completely wiped from the record). For example, why is the year expunged? Just secrecy reasons? Granted, all of these aren't necessarily bad expungements, they just strike me personally as odd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's the wiki code for the black box? I couldn't get that to work when writing. I'll change the DVDs thing as soon as I can.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You use a ton of [DATA EXPUNGED] where you could (and I believe should) have blackboxes- dates, names, that sort of thing- and "DVD's" should be DVDs and I can't stand that.
Also, although I like the effect, it feels like the cause is just creeeepy doll + creeeepy movie so spooky. And I don't think most police reports would include "Also the suspect watched a DVD the other day."

Edit: Tweaks and general use of feedback get a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …And for the editing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for catching the ##- I thought I got all of them. Could you elaborate on the improper interview format and weird expungement?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This definitely has some errors such as a leftover SCP-##, weird expungement, and improper interview formatting, but I liked the core concept enough that I upvoted. It totally has nothing to do with the fact that this is basically what I thought about when I was a little kid 24/7

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hello! New SCP, please read and review, but not until I get the picture up.
